title: Engineering across energy, catering & generative AI
summary: At adesso SE I've grown into a Senior Software Engineer and AI Expert, leading UI architecture and bridging design and development across very different domains — from enterprise energy dispatch to catering platforms — while driving the adoption of generative AI throughout the company.
responsibilities: I lead UI architecture and design-system work with Angular, PrimeNG, Tailwind CSS and Telerik/Kendo
I bridge the gap between design and engineering teams as the go-to person for UI/UX
I drive generative-AI enablement and modern DevOps practices
I coach engineers on UI/UX excellence and support technical hiring

children.title: Generative-AI enablement at scale
children.summary: I direct generative-AI enablement on the adessoGPT initiative, helping teams across the company adopt AI productively while keeping DevOps and delivery practices solid.
responsibilities: I enable generative-AI adoption across teams and use cases
I shape DevOps and delivery practices for the platform
I prototype and evaluate AI-assisted engineering workflows

children.title: Modernising the catering suite (MaxxLive · MaxxConference · MaxxApp)
children.summary: I work on Gantner DigiRest's catering suite — Angular web apps (MaxxLive, MaxxConference) and a Flutter mobile app (MaxxApp) for canteen ordering and balance management — modernising the tech stack and the experience.
responsibilities: I implement customer requirements and improve the tech stack
I integrate and rework payment providers (e.g. Adyen)
I drive design updates and the rebrand from contidata to Gantner
I maintain the cross-platform MaxxApp

children.title: Re-architecting enterprise energy dispatch (BxTrack)
children.summary: On BxTrack — the central dispatch hub for field operations in the energy sector — I took on the UI Lead role and steered the migration of a legacy ASP.NET MVC portal to a modern Blazor Server architecture, designing the multi-tenancy concept along the way.
responsibilities: I led the UI migration from ASP.NET MVC to Blazor Server with Telerik & MudBlazor
I designed the multi-tenancy capability across master-data and order management
I operated AWS infrastructure (RDS, EKS, ECR) provisioned entirely as Terraform IaC
I established UI quality gates and supported hiring through interviews

title: Building the ITM suite for the church market
summary: I started my career at ECKD building production software for the church/municipal registry market — owning both front- and backend across the ITM product suite, with the standardised XMeld registry data exchange at its core.
responsibilities: I developed front- and backend features across the ITM suite
I built and maintained CI/CD pipelines on Kubernetes with Helm
I supported requirements engineering and UI/UX design
I worked agile (Scrum/Kanban) with Git and GitFlow

children.title: Mastering the XMeld registry standard
children.summary: I engineered the XMeld processing module at the heart of ITM.KirA — turning high-volume XML registry feeds into a reliable single source of truth and aggregating fragmented person records into the highest-possible data correctness.
responsibilities: I upgraded the XMeld processing to XMeld 3.0 with gross-message (Bruttonachrichten) handling
I aggregated competing person-data sources into authoritative records
I resolved complex individual registry cases in support consultations
I analysed Big Data & AI opportunities in the ecclesiastical domain

children.title: Campaign landing pages & e-marketing
children.summary: I built a collection of landing pages for product launches, digitalisation campaigns and event venues, wiring them up to e-marketing tooling and the deployment pipeline.
responsibilities: I developed landing pages and connected them to e-marketing tools
I built CI/CD pipelines and supported deployment

children.title: Planning tool for parish work
children.summary: I developed the ITM.Gemeindeplaner end-to-end — a tool to help parishes plan and manage services, agendas and resources — owning the UI and supporting requirements management.
responsibilities: I developed the software front- and backend
I designed and implemented the UI
I supported requirements management and maintained the CI/CD pipelines

children.title: First steps: the church workplace platform
children.summary: I joined the ITM.KirA platform — the all-in-one tool for parishes to manage members, ceremonies, donations and elections — developing both front- and backend from day one.
responsibilities: I developed the software front- and backend
I supported requirements management

title: Business Informatics meets production code
summary: I combined academic rigour with hands-on delivery — a vocational apprenticeship in application development running in parallel with a Bachelor of Science in Business Informatics, so I could get the best out of both worlds.
responsibilities: I earned a B.Sc. in Business Informatics (specialisation: E-Business Management)
I completed an apprenticeship as an IT specialist for application development
I wrote my thesis on Big Data in the church registry domain (grade 1.3)

children.title: Bachelor of Science, Business Informatics
children.summary: I studied Business Informatics with a specialisation in E-Business Management and wrote my thesis on the potential of Big Data within the church registry domain (grade 1.3, colloquium 1.0).
responsibilities: Specialisation: E-Business Management
Thesis: Big Data in the church registry domain (grade 1.3)

children.title: IT Specialist for Application Development
children.summary: In parallel with my studies I completed a vocational apprenticeship as an IT specialist for application development, shipping real features from the very beginning.
responsibilities: I learned application development hands-on while delivering production work
I built a broad foundation across C#/.NET and web technologies
